objective: Implement the P0 GAP-001 trust-boundary slice by making codex preflight legacy positional invocation run the same required Local Memory and Project Brain readiness lane as flag-based required mode, preventing pass-looking preflight output when memory readiness was silently disabled.
ecosystemReviewFindings: Runtime readiness must be represented as structured evidence instead of a prose or pass-looking command summary.
Memory and runtime context are claim-supporting only when current and explicitly checked; stale or unavailable context can orient work but cannot support closeout.
deepModuleBoundary: Codex preflight script/template contract and its scaffold tests only. This slice is a trust-boundary fix for preflight readiness, not a new runtime-card, delivery-truth, or Project Brain feature.
implementationThesis: The legacy positional script path is still an executable preflight entrypoint, so it must not carry weaker readiness semantics than the documented required-mode path. Preserve compatibility for repo-fragment, bins, and paths positional arguments while defaulting the Local Memory lane to required; explicit --mode optional or --mode off remain deliberate operator choices through the flag-based interface.

legacyCompatibilityDecision.reason: This keeps parent-repo positional callers working while removing the implicit readiness downgrade. Fail-with-usage is rejected for this slice because it would trade a false-success fix for a compatibility break without evidence that legacy callers can be removed safely.
legacyCompatibilityDecision.operatorDowngradePath: Use the flag-based interface with --mode optional or --mode off when a weaker Local Memory lane is intentionally required.

currentEvidence.observed: usage text says legacy positional mode runs with Local Memory disabled unless the flag-based mode is used.
currentEvidence.gap: main() sets local_memory_mode='off' when the first argument is positional, so a legacy call can pass without Local Memory/Project Brain readiness.

currentEvidence.observed: the emitted downstream template contains the same legacy-disabled usage text and parser behavior.
currentEvidence.gap: downstream harness init scaffolding would preserve the same false-success path unless the template is synchronized.

currentEvidence.observed: scaffold assertions already pin required-mode defaults and the legacy compatibility text.
currentEvidence.gap: tests do not yet assert that legacy positional compatibility keeps Local Memory required instead of disabled.

currentEvidence.observed: GAP-001 identifies legacy preflight Local Memory downgrade as a P0 false-success trust-boundary gap.
currentEvidence.gap: the gap remains live in the script and template.

inScope: scripts/sync-codex-preflight.cjs only if the existing sync check requires no behavioral change
outOfScope: changing the Local Memory preflight implementation itself
changing Project Brain CLI behavior or knowledge-domain files unless a validation failure proves the preflight path requires it
adding a new public harness command
changing runtime-card, delivery-truth, pr-closeout, review-state, or external-state packet schemas
external writes to GitHub, Linear, CI, or review services during implementation
absorbing unrelated dirty worktree changes into this slice
acceptanceCriteria: Legacy positional invocation preserves repo-fragment, bins CSV, and paths CSV compatibility while leaving local_memory_mode at required by default.
Legacy positional invocation is evidence-equivalent to --mode required unless the operator explicitly uses the flag-based --mode optional or --mode off downgrade path.
Usage text no longer says legacy positional mode disables Local Memory.
The emitted src/templates/codex-preflight.sh remains byte-for-byte synchronized with scripts/codex-preflight.sh according to the repo sync check.
Scaffold tests fail if the template reintroduces local_memory_mode='off' for legacy positional parsing or documents a disabled default.
A focused behavior check demonstrates that a positional legacy invocation still attempts the Local Memory preflight unless an explicit flag-based optional/off mode is used.
Explicit --mode optional and --mode off remain valid flag-based choices and are not hidden behind positional compatibility.
Goal receipt records Project Brain inputs, GAP-001, ecosystem review alignment, validation commands, and any blocker.

automationPlan.name: intent artifact JSON validation
proves: slice intent is syntactically valid before review

automationPlan.name: pre-implementation intent review gate
proves: required intent review artifacts exist before implementation

automationPlan.name: codex-preflight script/template sync
proves: runtime preflight script and emitted downstream template remain synchronized

automationPlan.name: scaffold contract regression
proves: harness init scaffolding keeps the required Local Memory semantics

automationPlan.name: legacy positional behavior regression
proves: legacy positional invocation reaches the required Local Memory readiness lane instead of silently mapping to off
repo-fragment, bins CSV, and paths CSV compatibility survive the hardening change

automationPlan.name: required preflight smoke
proves: required preflight still exercises the live readiness lane

automationPlan.name: goal board validation
proves: goal state and receipts remain valid after slice updates

reviewQuestions: Does this intent stay tight enough to fix GAP-001 without drifting into broader Project Brain or runtime-evidence implementation?
Does the chosen map-to-required compatibility behavior preserve useful legacy arguments while preventing a pass-looking Local Memory skip?
Are the validation gates sufficient to catch script/template/test drift?

reviewFindingsAddressed.finding: Acceptance criterion for legacy-path behavior was not bound to a deterministic gate command.
reviewFindingsAddressed.resolution: Added legacy positional behavior regression command to automationPlan.

reviewFindingsAddressed.finding: Legacy semantics decision was dual-pathed between map-to-required and fail-with-usage.
reviewFindingsAddressed.resolution: Froze policy to map positional invocation to required Local Memory and documented explicit operator downgrade via --mode optional/off.

reviewFindingsAddressed.finding: Re-review verdict passed with no blocker/high findings.
reviewFindingsAddressed.resolution: Implementation may start against the reviewed map-to-required policy and legacy positional behavior regression gate.

reviewFindingsAddressed.finding: Re-review verdict passed with no blocker/high findings.
reviewFindingsAddressed.resolution: Agent-native execution readiness is sufficient for the narrow implementation slice.

postReviewMutationPolicy: any material mutation to objective, inScope, outOfScope, acceptanceCriteria, automationPlan, reviewPlan, stopConditions, or rollback resets reviewStatus to pending
linearMutationPlan: No external Linear mutation during this slice implementation. JSC-363 remains linked in the intent and receipt; a Linear comment may be added after local validation if connector availability is confirmed.
stopConditions: Stop if implementation evidence proves map-to-required breaks a documented parent-repo compatibility path that cannot be resolved without broader migration approval.
Stop if a test cannot distinguish explicit --mode off from implicit positional Local Memory disablement.
Stop if the sync check indicates scripts/codex-preflight.sh is not the canonical source for src/templates/codex-preflight.sh.
Stop if required preflight cannot run and no env-loaded recovery or explicit blocker is recorded.
Stop if implementation would touch unrelated Project Brain, agent-readiness, runtime-card, delivery-truth, or external-state files.
rollback: Revert script/template/test edits if required Local Memory mode breaks a documented parent-repo compatibility path, then pause for an explicit migration decision before replacing positional compatibility with fail-with-usage behavior.
Keep the regression that prevents implicit local_memory_mode='off' unless a stronger fail-closed parser replaces it.
Do not roll back unrelated dirty worktree changes.
assumptions: The root scripts/codex-preflight.sh file is the canonical source synchronized into src/templates/codex-preflight.sh.
Legacy positional arguments are still used by parent-repo checks and should remain accepted when they do not weaken readiness.
Required Local Memory preflight includes the current Project Brain readiness surface through PROJECT_BRAIN_REQUIRED_PATHS and local-memory-preflight.
JSC-363 remains the Linear owner for this lifecycle goal.
